revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 聖現寺 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那覇市}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[上之屋]]392 |位置 = |山号 = 天久山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[観音菩薩|正観音菩薩]] |創建年 = [[成化]]年間（[[1465年]] - [[1487年]]） |開山 = 咄海禅師 |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 天久の寺（アマクヌティラ） |札所…」
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 聖現寺 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那覇市}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[上之屋]]392 |位置 = |山号 = 天久山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[観音菩薩|正観音菩薩]] |創建年 = [[成化]]年間（[[1465年]] - [[1487年]]） |開山 = 咄海禅師 |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 天久の寺（アマクヌティラ） |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''聖現寺'''（せいげんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。
== 歴史 == [[成化]]年間（[[1465年]] - [[1487年]]）、咄海禅師によって[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された。当初は[[臨済宗]]の寺であったが、[[1671年]]（[[康熙]]10年）に頼昌法印によって[[真言宗]]に転宗した。[[1734年]]（[[雍正]]12年）に[[天久宮]]を移設した。当寺は天久宮の[[別当寺]]でもある<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 聖現寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」</ref><ref name="百科">沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖繩タイムス社、1983年、546p</ref>。
[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けた。[[1958年]]に再建された<ref name="百科"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]上之屋停留所より徒歩8分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいけんし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 聖現寺 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那覇市}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[上之屋]]392 |位置 = {{Coord|26|13|42.5|N|127|40|56.1|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 天久山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[観音菩薩|正観音菩薩]] |創建年 = [[成化]]年間（[[1465年]] - [[1487年]]） |開山 = 咄海禅師 |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 天久の寺（アマクヌティラ） |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''聖現寺'''（せいげんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。
== 歴史 == [[成化]]年間（[[1465年]] - [[1487年]]）、咄海禅師によって[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された。当初は[[臨済宗]]の寺であったが、[[1671年]]（[[康熙]]10年）に頼昌法印によって[[真言宗]]に転宗した。[[1734年]]（[[雍正]]12年）に[[天久宮]]を移設した。当寺は天久宮の[[別当寺]]でもある<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 聖現寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」</ref><ref name="百科">沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖繩タイムス社、1983年、546p</ref>。
[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けた。[[1958年]]に再建された<ref name="百科"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]上之屋停留所より徒歩8分。

== 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年

revision.comment: Created page with " {{ill|Seigen-ji|ja|聖現寺}} ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en}} ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|ja|遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)}}) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine}} (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮}} (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世..."
revision.text:
{{ill|Seigen-ji|ja|聖現寺}} ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en}} ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|ja|遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)}}) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine}} (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮}} (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮}} would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|ja|琉球八社}}.
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|ja|金武宮}} which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|ja|金武観音寺}} and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|ja|琉球八社}}.
{{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]]

{{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].
{{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]]

{{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].
{{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Seigen-ji]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 聖現寺 |画像 = [[File:Seigen-ji (Naha).JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[上之屋]]392 |位置 = {{Coord|26|13|42.5|N|127|40|56.1|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 天久山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[観音菩薩|正観音菩薩]] |創建年 = [[成化]]年間（[[1465年]] - [[1487年]]） |開山 = 咄海禅師 |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 天久の寺（アマクヌティラ） |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''聖現寺'''（せいげんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。
== 歴史 == [[成化]]年間（[[1465年]] - [[1487年]]）、咄海禅師によって[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された。当初は[[臨済宗]]の寺であったが、[[1671年]]（[[康熙]]10年）に頼昌法印によって[[真言宗]]に転宗した。[[1734年]]（[[雍正]]12年）に[[天久宮]]を移設した。当寺は天久宮の[[別当寺]]でもある<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 聖現寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」</ref><ref name="百科">沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖繩タイムス社、1983年、546p</ref>。
[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けた。[[1958年]]に再建された<ref name="百科"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]上之屋停留所より徒歩8分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいけんし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].

{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいけんし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].
{{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:eigen-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:eigen-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Seigen-ji]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Seigen-ji]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 聖現寺 |image = [[File:Seigen-ji (Naha).JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[上之屋]]392 |位置 = {{Coord|26|13|42.5|N|127|40|56.1|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 天久山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[観音菩薩|正観音菩薩]] |創建年 = [[成化]]年間（[[1465]] - [[1487]]） |開山 = 咄海禅師 |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 天久の寺（アマクヌティラ） |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''聖現寺'''（せいげんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。
== 歴史 == [[成化]]年間（[[1465]] - [[1487]]）、咄海禅師によって[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された。当初は[[臨済宗]]の寺であったが、[[1671]]（[[康熙]]10年）に頼昌法印によって[[真言宗]]に転宗した。[[1734]]（[[雍正]]12年）に[[天久宮]]を移設した。当寺は天久宮の[[別当寺]]でもある<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 聖現寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」</ref><ref name="百科">沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖繩タイムス社、1983年、546p</ref>。
[[1945]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けた。[[1958]]に再建された<ref name="百科"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]上之屋停留所より徒歩8分。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいけんし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].
{{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:eigen-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:eigen-ji]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Seigen-ji]][[sn:Seigen-ji]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 聖現寺 |image = [[File:Seigen-ji (Naha).JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[上之屋]]392 |位置 = {{Coord|26|13|42.5|N|127|40|56.1|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 天久山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[観音菩薩|正観音菩薩]] |創建年 = [[成化]]年間（[[1465]] - [[1487]]） |開山 = 咄海禅師 |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 天久の寺（アマクヌティラ） |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''聖現寺'''（せいげんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。
== 歴史 == [[成化]]年間（[[1465]] - [[1487]]）、咄海禅師によって[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された。当初は[[臨済宗]]の寺であったが、[[1671]]（[[康熙]]10年）に頼昌法印によって[[真言宗]]に転宗した。[[1734]]（[[雍正]]12年）に[[天久宮]]を移設した。当寺は天久宮の[[別当寺]]でもある<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 聖現寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」</ref><ref name="百科">沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖繩タイムス社、1983年、546p</ref>。
[[1945]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けた。[[1958]]に再建された<ref name="百科"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]上之屋停留所より徒歩8分。

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 聖現寺 |image = [[File:Seigen-ji (Naha).JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[上之屋]]392 |位置 = {{Coord|26|13|42.5|N|127|40|56.1|E|region:JP-47_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 天久山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[東寺真言宗]] |本尊 = [[観音菩薩|正観音菩薩]] |創建年 = [[成化]]年間（[[1465]] - [[1487]]） |開山 = 咄海禅師 |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 天久の寺（アマクヌティラ） |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |}} '''聖現寺'''（せいげんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]にある[[東寺真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。
== 歴史 == [[成化]]年間（[[1465]] - [[1487]]）、咄海禅師によって[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された。当初は[[臨済宗]]の寺であったが、[[1671]]（[[康熙]]10年）に頼昌法印によって[[真言宗]]に転宗した。[[1734]]（[[雍正]]12年）に[[天久宮]]を移設した。当寺は天久宮の[[別当寺]]でもある<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 聖現寺]那覇市「那覇市観光資源データベース」</ref><ref name="百科">沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖繩タイムス社、1983年、546p</ref>。
[[1945]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[沖縄戦]]で壊滅的打撃を受けた。[[1958]]に再建された<ref name="百科"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]上之屋停留所より徒歩8分。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *沖繩大百科事典刊行事務局 編『沖繩大百科事典 中巻：ケ-ト』沖縄タイムス社、1983年
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいけんし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:東寺真言宗の寺院]] {{ill|Seigen-ji|en}}[[Category:U]][[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seigen-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:_completion_categories]] [[ja:聖現寺]] ), # {{ill|10=ja|11=末吉宮|Sueyoshi-gū|8=en]] ({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en}}[[Category:U]][[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seigen-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:_completion_categories]] [[ja:遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)]]) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:U]][[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seigen-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:_completion_categories]] [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine]] and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en}}[[Category:U]][[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seigen-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:_completion_categories]] [[ja:長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮]] would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:U]][[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seigen-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:_completion_categories]] [[ja:琉球八社]].
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en}}[[Category:U]][[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seigen-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:_completion_categories]] [[ja:金武宮]] which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:U]][[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seigen-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:_completion_categories]] [[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:U]][[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seigen-ji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:_completion_categories]] [[ja:琉球八社]].

'''Seigen-ji''' (聖現寺, せいげんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|qq=|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|qq=|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], it converted to the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|qq=|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|qq=|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==
* An 8‑minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

== References ==

{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Kankō Jimukyoku, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

{{buddhism-stub}}

{{Okinawan Shrines}}

 {{ill|Seigen-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigen-ji|qq=}}

# {{ill|Sueyoshi-gū|ja|末吉宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueyoshi-gū|qq=}} (see also {{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|qq=}})
# {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} (Choju-ji Temple), and
# {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}}

 (Choraku-ji Temple)
Later on, all Zenke shrines except for {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}} would convert to [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}.

Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin-gū|qq=}}

, which, although quite small, was always affiliated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin Kannon-ji|qq=}},

 and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}

'''Seigen-ji''' (聖現寺, せいげんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|qq=|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|qq=|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], it converted to the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|qq=|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|qq=|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==
* An 8‑minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Kankō Jimukyoku, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

{{Okinawan Shrines}}

 {{ill|Seigen-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigen-ji|qq=}}

# {{ill|Sueyoshi-gū|ja|末吉宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueyoshi-gū|qq=}} (see also {{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|qq=}})
# {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} (Choju-ji Temple), and
# {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}}

 (Choraku-ji Temple)
Later on, all Zenke shrines except for {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}} would convert to [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}.

Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin-gū|qq=}}

, which, although quite small, was always affiliated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin Kannon-ji|qq=}},

 and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}

'''Seigen-ji''' (聖現寺, せいげんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], it converted to the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==
* An 8‑minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Kankō Jimukyoku, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

{{Okinawan Shrines}}

 {{ill|Seigen-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigen-ji|qq=}}

# {{ill|Sueyoshi-gū|ja|末吉宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueyoshi-gū|qq=}} (see also {{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|qq=}})
# {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} (Choju-ji Temple), and
# {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}}

 (Choraku-ji Temple)
Later on, all Zenke shrines except for {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}} would convert to [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}.

Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin-gū|qq=}}

, which, although quite small, was always affiliated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin Kannon-ji|qq=}},

 and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}

'''Seigen-ji''' (聖現寺, せいげんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], it converted to the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==
* An 8‑minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

{{Okinawan Shrines}}

 {{ill|Seigen-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigen-ji|qq=}}

# {{ill|Sueyoshi-gū|ja|末吉宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueyoshi-gū|qq=}} (see also {{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|qq=}})
# {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} (Choju-ji Temple), and
# {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}}

 (Choraku-ji Temple)
Later on, all Zenke shrines except for {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}} would convert to [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}.

Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin-gū|qq=}}

, which, although quite small, was always affiliated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin Kannon-ji|qq=}},

 and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}

Originally established as a temple of the [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], it converted to the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==
* An 8‑minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

{{Okinawan Shrines}}

 {{ill|Seigen-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigen-ji|qq=}}

# {{ill|Sueyoshi-gū|ja|末吉宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueyoshi-gū|qq=}} (see also {{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|qq=}})
# {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} (Choju-ji Temple), and
# {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}}

 (Choraku-ji Temple)
Later on, all Zenke shrines except for {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}} would convert to [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}.

Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin-gū|qq=}}

, which, although quite small, was always affiliated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin Kannon-ji|qq=}},

 and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], it converted to the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==
* An 8‑minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

'''Seigen-ji''' (聖現寺, せいげんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], it converted to the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==

According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

'''Seigen-ji''' (聖現寺, せいげんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai Sect]], it converted to the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.
It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>
== Access == * An 8‑minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Kankō Jimukyoku, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

{{Okinawan Shrines}}
{{ill|Seigen-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigen-ji|qq=}}
# {{ill|Sueyoshi-gū|ja|末吉宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueyoshi-gū|qq=}} (see also {{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|qq=}}) # {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}}
(Choraku-ji Temple) Later on, all Zenke shrines except for {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}} would convert to [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}.
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin-gū|qq=}}
, which, although quite small, was always affiliated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin Kannon-ji|qq=}},
and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}

'''Seigen-ji''' (聖現寺, せいげんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Naha|Naha City]].

This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.
It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>
== Access == * An 8‑minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

(Choraku-ji Temple) Later on, all Zenke shrines except for {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}} would convert to {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}.
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin-gū|qq=}}
, which, although quite small, was always affiliated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhist}} temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin Kannon-ji|qq=}},
and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}

'''Seigen-ji''' (聖現寺, せいげんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha City}}.

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai Sect}}, it converted to the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect}} in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==
* An 8‑minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Kankō Jimukyoku, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

Later on, all Zenke shrines except for {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}} would convert to {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}.

Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin-gū|qq=}}

, which, although quite small, was always affiliated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhist}} temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin Kannon-ji|qq=}},

 and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}

According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai Sect}}, it converted to the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect}} in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==
* An 8‑minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

== References ==

{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", edited by the Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten Kankō Jimukyoku, Okinawa Times-sha, 1983

{{buddhism-stub}}

{{Okinawan Shrines}}

 {{ill|Seigen-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigen-ji|qq=}}

# {{ill|Sueyoshi-gū|ja|末吉宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueyoshi-gū|qq=}} (see also {{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|qq=}})
# {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} (Choju-ji Temple), and
# {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}}

 (Choraku-ji Temple)
Later on, all Zenke shrines except for {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}} would convert to {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}.

Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin-gū|qq=}}

, which, although quite small, was always affiliated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhist}} temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin Kannon-ji|qq=}},

 and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}

'''Seigen-ji''' (聖現寺, せいげんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha City}}.

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai Sect}}, it converted to the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect}} in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==
* An 8-minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==

'''Seigen-ji''' (聖現寺, せいげんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon Sect|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon Sect|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10343918}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha City|1=Naha}}.

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Seika|ja|成化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seika|1=Chenghua|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061787}} period (1465–1487), the temple was founded (kaizan) by {{ill|Dokukai Zenji|ja|咄海禅師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dokukai Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Originally established as a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai Sect|1=Rinzai school}}, it converted to the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}} in 1671 (the 10th year of the {{ill|Kangxi|ja|康熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangxi|1=Kangxi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185178}} era) under the auspices of {{ill|Raichō Hōin|ja|頼昌法印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichō Hōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in 1734 (the 12th year of the {{ill|Yongzheng|ja|雍正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongzheng|1=Yongzheng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q708977}} era) the Tenkyū-gu was relocated. This temple also serves as the bettō (administrative temple) of Tenkyū-gu.<ref name="観光">[https://www.naha-contentsdb.jp/spot/443 "Seigen-ji"] Naha City "Naha City Tourism Resource Database"</ref><ref name="百科">Okinawa Daihyakka Jiten, Vol.2: "Ke-To", published by Okinawa Times-sha, 1983, p.546</ref>.

It suffered catastrophic damage during the Battle of Okinawa in 1945 (Showa 20) and was rebuilt in 1958.<ref name="百科"></ref>

== Access ==
* An 8-minute walk from the Uenoya bus stop (【路線バス】).

{{Okinawan Shrines}}

 {{ill|Seigen-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigen-ji|qq=}}

# {{ill|Sueyoshi-gū|ja|末吉宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueyoshi-gū|qq=}} (see also {{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|qq=}})
# {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} (Choju-ji Temple), and
# {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}}

 (Choraku-ji Temple)
Later on, all Zenke shrines except for {{ill|Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|ja|世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomochi and Ukishima Shrines#Ukishima Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choraku-ji Tenmangū|qq=}} would convert to {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}.

Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin-gū|qq=}}

, which, although quite small, was always affiliated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhist|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin Kannon-ji|qq=}},

 and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ryukyu Shrines|qq=}}
